mapping.a_element: local measurements (projections in the von Neumann algebra) recorded along the observer's worldline
mapping.b_element: data points obtained from a Takens embedding of S&P 500 returns

mapping.a_element: simplicial complex constructed out of the recorded measurements
mapping.b_element: Vietoris-Rips simplicial complex built from the point cloud

mapping.a_element: topological information the complex captures about spacetime
mapping.b_element: Betti numbers extracted via combinatorial Laplacians / null-space counting

mapping.a_element: the underlying (fluctuating) spacetime whose topology is probed
mapping.b_element: the underlying manifold/attractor of the market time series being characterized

brief_justification: Both build a simplicial complex from a discrete set of observations and read off its homology/Betti numbers to characterize the topology of an underlying, possibly fluctuating space — the machinery of simplicial topological data analysis. Paper A does this from worldline measurements to probe spacetime topology; paper B does it via a Vietoris-Rips filtration and combinatorial Laplacians to probe the topology of financial time-series data, so a coherent role-for-role mapping holds despite the quantum-gravity vs quantum-computing/finance domains.
